interpretation: Fixture dry-run result is advisory only; collect 20 live browser tasks before changing the Browser roadmap branch.

firstOptInEvidence.note: First opt-in run is end-to-end chain proof with a stub adapter. After D-118 batch run + D-119 real fetch + D-120 hybrid evidence + D-124 updatedLedger accumulation, the cumulative live result count is 13/20. D-121 fixes non-contiguous hybrid task mapping, D-122 adds per-task JS action mapping, D-123 makes hybrid evidence return a recomputed updatedLedger, and D-124 consumes that updatedLedger to advance the cumulative count by 4. 7/20 remain pending and binding remains false until 20/20 are recorded through real Browser automation in D-125+.

firstBatchEvidence.note: First batch opt-in run records 3 additional completed live results in 1 call. The function design supports batchSize up to 20 (which would flip binding to true), but the D-118 live ledger update only advances 1->4 to keep the binding claim honest with stub data.

firstRealEvidence.realFetchAdapter: Node built-in fetch (HTTP only, no JS execution)
firstRealEvidence.note: First real-evidence run uses Node built-in fetch to hit real public URLs and record real network-call evidence. The 2 fetched URLs returned 200 with real content (Example Domain / IANA). Of 6 cumulative completed live results, 4 are stub-evidence (D-117/118) and 2 are real-evidence (D-119). Binding remains false because 14/20 are still pending. D-119 does NOT claim puppeteer / Chromium / JS-execution parity; it claims real HTTP-level evidence (real network call, real status, real title, real response body). D-119 adds zero new npm deps.

firstHybridEvidence.hybridAdapter: HTTP fetch plus JS-rendered form interaction evidence
firstHybridEvidence.note: D-120 records 3 additional hybrid evidence rows on top of the 6 D-117..D-119 rows, bringing repository live result accounting to 9/20. D-121 fixes non-contiguous hybrid task mapping, D-122 adds per-task JS action mapping, and D-123 makes hybrid evidence return a recomputed updatedLedger for future hybrid evidence batches, without increasing the completed live-result count. D-124 then consumes that updatedLedger to advance the cumulative count from 9/20 to 13/20 (see latestHybridEvidence). After D-124, 4 are stub-evidence (D-117/118), 6 are HTTP-evidence (D-119/120/124), and 3 are JS-evidence (D-120/124). Binding remains false because 7/20 are still pending, so Browser defaults remain locked.

latestHybridEvidence.hybridAdapter: HTTP fetch plus JS-rendered form interaction evidence (updatedLedger accumulation)
latestHybridEvidence.note: D-124 chains two updatedLedger accumulation runs of recordHybridRealBrowserEvidence to record 4 additional live evidence results on top of the 9 from D-117..D-120, advancing repository live result accounting from 9/20 to 13/20. Of the 13 cumulative completed live results, 4 are stub-evidence (D-117/118), 6 are HTTP-evidence (D-119/120/124), and 3 are JS-evidence (D-120/124). Binding remains false because 7/20 are still pending, so Browser defaults remain locked. D-124 advances evidence but does not change the binding threshold or default registry exposure.
